title: Meta Platforms rises 10% after announcing a cloud business to sell its excess AI capacity
summary: The technology company plans to lease its artificial intelligence infrastructure to third parties, boosting its shares and sinking the stock prices of competitors such as Nebius, Coreweave, and IREN.

title: Chip makers plummet after OpenAI's efficiency improvement reduces demand for semiconductors
summary: The SOX semiconductor index falls 5%, Nvidia retreats to key levels, and the chip sector leads losses on Wall Street.

title: Fed President Kevin Warsh suggests inflation is easing and avoids committing to future rate hikes
summary: In his international debut in Sintra, Warsh indicated that inflation risks have diminished and that the debate on rates will continue, generating relief in markets.

title: Lime debuts on Nasdaq at $27 per share with a valuation of $1.73 billion
summary: The shared mobility company backed by Uber raises $167 million in its IPO, considered timely.

title: European stock markets close lower on the first day of July, with the IBEX 35 falling 0.34%
summary: The IBEX 35, FTSE 100, and PSI 20 record declines in a session marked by caution ahead of the Fed's speech and employment data.

title: Eurozone inflation falls to 2.8% in June and Lagarde rules out further rate hikes
summary: The year-on-year CPI moderates, the ECB president sees less inflationary risk, and Euribor reflects expectations of stable rates.

title: Gold heads for its worst quarter in over a decade amid declining retail interest
summary: The price of the precious metal continues to fall, with expectations that the bottom has not yet been reached, according to analysts.

title: Trump earned more than $1 billion in 2025, including millions in cryptocurrency income
summary: The president's financial disclosure reveals income exceeding $1 billion, with significant gains in the crypto asset market.

title: Yen pressures Japanese retail stocks and the market speculates about currency intervention
summary: The yen's weakness continues to affect Japanese companies, while 'Mr. Yen' maintains uncertainty about a possible intervention by the Bank of Japan.

title: Wall Street closes its best quarter since 2020 with strong gains driven by AI
summary: The S&P 500 records its biggest quarterly gain in six years, supported by optimism in artificial intelligence and expectations of rate cuts.

title: Palantir Technologies surges after being rated as a leader in business intelligence by analysts
summary: Palantir shares soar after Wolfe Research highlighted its dominant position in corporate artificial intelligence, despite its high valuation.

title: Wall Street starts third quarter with mixed results: Dow rises, Nasdaq falls
summary: The first day of July shows a positive Dow Jones but a Nasdaq weighed down by tech stocks, as investors digest the Fed's statements.

title: Memory demand fuels optimism in chip makers like Micron
summary: Analysts suggest that Micron could reach $2,000 if memory demand continues, while investors debate whether chips are the most attractive sector.

title: Asian markets cool slightly but foreign investors continue buying Japanese stocks
summary: The Nikkei and Kospi moderate their advance, while foreign investors increase their exposure to Japan due to its attractive valuation and structural reforms.

title: Acciona Energía to build new solar plant in Kentucky (USA) as part of its international expansion
summary: The Spanish company has announced the construction of a new photovoltaic facility in the United States, strengthening its presence in the North American market.

title: Sony to end sales of disc-based games for PlayStation in 2028
summary: The Japanese company plans to cease physical distribution of new video game titles, betting on the digital format.

title: Japanese prime minister visits India and announces 10 trillion yen investment in economic and technological cooperation
summary: During his visit, an investment package in areas of economic security and artificial intelligence was agreed.

title: Alcoa acquires alumina and bauxite assets from South32 for $4.8 billion
summary: The US mining company strengthens its aluminum business with the purchase of strategic assets from Australia's South32.

title: Getty cancels merger with Shutterstock valued at $3.7 billion after British authority veto
summary: The consolidation operation in the stock image market is abandoned after the UK regulator blocked it.

title: Binance and its founder are sued by crypto investors in a London court
summary: The investors allege millions in losses and accuse the crypto asset exchange platform of breaching regulations.
